title: Manager creates employee
statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to create a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

title: Manager updates employee
statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

title: Submit leave request
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 2
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 3
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 4
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Submit leave request — variant 5
statement: A full-time employee shall be able to submit a leave request for between 1 and 30 business days; requests spanning more than 30 days require hr-admin approval. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
error_pathways: 422 when request exceeds 30 days without hr-admin

title: Approve leave request
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin.

title: Approve leave request — variant 2
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Approve leave request — variant 3
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Approve leave request — variant 4
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Approve leave request — variant 5
statement: A manager shall approve or reject leave requests assigned to them within 5 business days; unactioned requests auto-escalate to hr-admin. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Submit timesheet
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 2
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 3
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 4
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Submit timesheet — variant 5
statement: An employee shall submit a weekly timesheet where each day has hours between 0 and 24 and total week hours are between 0 and 80. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Run payroll
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 2
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 3
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 4
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Run payroll — variant 5
statement: An hr-admin shall run a monthly payroll that computes gross pay, deductions, and net pay for each active employee; a failed run shall not partially commit. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.
error_pathways: 409 if another run is in progress

title: Upload onboarding document
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected.

title: Upload onboarding document — variant 2
statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Upload onboarding document — variant 3

statement: An employee shall upload onboarding documents up to 10MB in PDF, PNG, or JPG format; files outside this must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Export employee directory
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory.

title: Export employee directory — variant 2
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Export employee directory — variant 3
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Export employee directory — variant 4
statement: An hr-admin shall export the employee directory as CSV with PII redacted for employees who opted out of public directory. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Export employee directory — variant 5

title: Announcement rollout
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 2
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 3
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 4
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Announcement rollout — variant 5
statement: A system-admin shall publish an org-wide announcement to employees in chosen departments; publishing without any department selection shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Schedule performance review
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 2
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 3
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 4
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Schedule performance review — variant 5
statement: A manager shall schedule a performance review with the reviewee at least 7 days in advance; past or sub-7-day dates shall be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Bulk import employees
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 2
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 3
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 4
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Bulk import employees — variant 5
statement: An hr-admin shall bulk-import employees via CSV; the import shall validate each row and commit only rows that pass validation, reporting errors per row. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: Reset employee password
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected.

title: Reset employee password — variant 2
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must be idempotent under retry.

title: Reset employee password — variant 3
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action must emit a webhook event for external consumers.

title: Reset employee password — variant 4
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the operation must be rate-limited per user.

title: Reset employee password — variant 5
statement: An employee shall reset their password via an email link that expires after 30 minutes; expired links must be rejected. Additionally, the action requires two-factor authentication confirmation.

title: List employees pagination
statement: The employee list shall be paginated at 50 per page with cursor-based pagination.

title: Timezone handling
statement: Timestamps stored shall be UTC; UI shall render in user's configured timezone.
error_pathways: default to UTC when tz missing

title: Feature flag rollout
statement: An admin shall toggle feature flags per environment; toggles shall be audit-logged.

title: Webhook delivery
statement: Outbound webhooks shall retry on 5xx with exponential backoff up to 5 times; permanent failures shall be DLQ'd.

title: Session management
statement: A user session shall expire after 30 minutes of inactivity; expired sessions shall redirect to login.

title: Accessibility compliance
statement: All UI screens shall be keyboard-navigable and meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast ratios.

title: Open checking account
statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected.

title: Open savings account
statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically.

title: Transfer funds between own accounts
statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined.

title: External ACH transfer
statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification.

title: Recurring transfer
statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500.

title: Wire transfer
statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review.

title: Bill pay
statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off.

title: Account statement generation
statement: A customer shall request monthly statements as PDF; statements older than 7 years shall be fetched from cold storage.

title: Transaction dispute
statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected.

title: Card freeze / unfreeze
statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts.

title: Beneficiary management
statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers.

title: Overdraft protection
statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined.

title: Fraud alert response
statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account.

title: Account close
statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account.

title: KYC refresh
statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only.

title: Interest accrual
statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day.

title: Account-level transaction limits
statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down.

title: Joint account operations
statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

title: Cheque deposit by mobile
statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification.

title: Standing order
statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected.

title: Savings goal
statement: A customer shall create a savings goal with a target amount and target date; progress shall be computed nightly.

title: Card PIN change
statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window.

title: Biller registration
statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred.

title: Rate limiting
statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after.

title: Reconciliation report
statement: The system shall produce a daily reconciliation report comparing internal ledger to correspondent bank; non-zero variance shall open an incident.

title: Open checking account (boundary variant)
statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

title: Open savings account (boundary variant)
statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

title: Transfer funds between own accounts (boundary variant)
statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Boundary behaviour at exact min/max thresholds must be tested.

title: External ACH transfer (boundary variant)

title: Recurring transfer (boundary variant)

title: Wire transfer (boundary variant)

title: Open checking account (negative variant)
statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Open savings account (negative variant)
statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: External ACH transfer (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Recurring transfer (negative variant)
statement: A premium-tier customer shall be able to schedule a recurring transfer up to $10,000 per occurrence; non-premium accounts capped at $2,500. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Wire transfer (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall initiate a wire transfer requiring two-factor authentication; transfers above $100,000 require manual review. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Bill pay (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall pay a registered biller between $1 and $50,000 with deterministic settlement date calculation based on biller cut-off. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Account statement generation (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall request monthly statements as PDF; statements older than 7 years shall be fetched from cold storage. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Transaction dispute (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall dispute a transaction within 60 days of posting; disputes beyond 60 days shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Card freeze / unfreeze (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall freeze or unfreeze their card instantly; frozen cards must reject all authorization attempts. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Beneficiary management (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall add a beneficiary which enters a 24-hour cooling-off period before it can receive transfers. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Overdraft protection (negative variant)
statement: An overdraft shall trigger a draw from the linked savings account up to the linked account's available balance; otherwise the transaction shall be declined. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Fraud alert response (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall confirm or deny a suspected-fraud alert within 24 hours; unacknowledged alerts shall auto-freeze the account. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Account close (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: KYC refresh (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall complete a KYC refresh every 365 days; accounts beyond refresh window shall be restricted to inbound transactions only. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Interest accrual (negative variant)
statement: A savings account shall accrue interest daily using the tier rate and credit interest monthly on the last business day. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Account-level transaction limits (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Joint account operations (negative variant)
statement: A joint-account holder shall perform transactions; the secondary holder shall not be able to modify ownership. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

title: Cheque deposit by mobile (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Standing order (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Savings goal (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall create a savings goal with a target amount and target date; progress shall be computed nightly. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Card PIN change (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Biller registration (negative variant)
statement: A customer shall register a biller only after a 24-hour hold; withdrawals to new billers shall be deferred. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Rate limiting (negative variant)
statement: The API shall enforce 60 requests per minute per user; overages shall return 429 with retry-after. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Reconciliation report (negative variant)
statement: The system shall produce a daily reconciliation report comparing internal ledger to correspondent bank; non-zero variance shall open an incident. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.

title: Open checking account (idempotency variant)
statement: A verified customer shall be able to open a checking account with an initial deposit between $25 and $50,000; deposits outside this range shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: Open savings account (idempotency variant)
statement: A verified customer shall open a savings account with a minimum balance of $100 and tiered interest rates applied automatically. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: Transfer funds between own accounts (idempotency variant)
statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: External ACH transfer (idempotency variant)
statement: A customer shall transfer funds to an external ACH-linked account up to $10,000 per transfer; transfers above this shall require step-up verification. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

statement: A customer shall configure daily per-channel limits within regulatory caps; changes take effect after a 2-hour cool-down. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

error_pathways: 403 when secondary holder attempts ownership change

statement: A customer shall deposit a cheque via mobile image; cheques above $5,000 shall require manual verification. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: Standing order (idempotency variant)
statement: A customer shall set up a standing order with start date in the future; past start dates shall be rejected. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: Savings goal (idempotency variant)
statement: A customer shall create a savings goal with a target amount and target date; progress shall be computed nightly. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: Card PIN change (idempotency variant)
statement: A customer shall change their card PIN at most 5 times per 24-hour window. The operation must be idempotent: duplicate submission with the same Idempotency-Key must return the original result.

title: Create patient
statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected.

title: Read patient
statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied.

title: Update patient
statement: A clinician shall update patient demographics; historical versions must be preserved.

title: Delete patient (soft)
statement: A system-admin shall soft-delete a patient resource; deletion shall not remove history.

title: Create observation
statement: A clinician shall create an Observation tied to a patient with LOINC-coded code element; Observations without code shall be rejected.

title: Create encounter
statement: A clinician shall create an Encounter for a patient with start and end timestamps; end before start shall be rejected.

title: Close encounter
statement: A clinician shall close an Encounter; closed Encounters shall reject further modifications except for addendum Observations.

title: Create medication request
statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification.

title: Allergy intolerance
statement: A clinician shall record an AllergyIntolerance linked to the patient; subsequent MedicationRequests shall warn on conflict.

title: Immunization record
statement: A clinician shall record an Immunization with administered date not in the future.

title: Procedure record
statement: A clinician shall record a Procedure tied to an Encounter.

title: Diagnostic report
statement: A clinician shall issue a DiagnosticReport referencing Observations; missing Observation links shall be rejected.

title: Search patients
statement: A clinician shall search patients by name, MRN, or DOB; only patients in their care shall be returned.

title: Audit log access
statement: A privacy-officer shall access audit logs scoped to their organisation; cross-organisation access shall be forbidden.

title: Consent management
statement: A patient shall grant or revoke consent for data sharing; revoked consent shall be enforced within 15 minutes.
error_pathways: accesses after revoke within SLA denied

title: Bulk data export
statement: A provisioned client shall request a FHIR bulk export; large datasets shall be paginated with NDJSON output.

title: Inter-facility referral
statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check.

title: Lab order routing
statement: A clinician shall order a lab test; orders shall route to the patient's preferred lab when available.
error_pathways: fallback to default lab when preferred unavailable

title: Vital signs validation
statement: An Observation with category vital-signs shall have value in clinically plausible range; out-of-range shall be flagged for review.
expected_outcomes: flag raised if out of range

title: Data retention
statement: Resources shall be retained per policy; expired resources shall be purged on a nightly job with full audit trail.

title: Break-glass access
statement: A clinician shall invoke break-glass access for an emergent patient; break-glass use shall be audited and trigger a post-event review.

title: Device data ingest
statement: A registered device shall push Observations via FHIR ingest; unregistered devices shall be rejected.

title: Patient portal login
statement: A patient shall log in using SSO; failed attempts shall be rate-limited and notify the patient.

title: Appointment scheduling
statement: A patient shall schedule an appointment at least 24 hours in advance during provider's available slots.
error_pathways: 422 when slot unavailable or in past

title: Message to provider
statement: A patient shall send a message to their provider; messages containing attachments shall scan for disallowed PHI exfiltration patterns.

title: Create patient (consent)
statement: A registered clinician shall create a patient resource with validated demographics; creation with missing mandatory elements shall be rejected. Consent state at the time of the operation must be tested, including post-revocation timing windows.

title: Read patient (consent)
statement: A clinician shall read a patient resource for patients in their care; reading outside their assignment shall be denied. Consent state at the time of the operation must be tested, including post-revocation timing windows.
